exemplar.title: The Microeconomic Analysis of the Pumpkin Spice Phenomenon: Seasonal Demand and Consumer Preferences in Niche Market Demands

detailedExemplarData.title: The Microeconomic Analysis of the Pumpkin Spice Phenomenon: Seasonal Demand and Consumer Preferences in Niche Market Demands

subject.title: Economics
subject.description:
**IB Economics** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **microeconomics, macroeconomics, the global economy, and development economics**, with a strong focus on **analytical and evaluative skills, real-world application of economic theory, and quantitative analysis**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Extended response questions | 1h 15min | 1h 30min | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Data response questions | 1h 45min | 1h 45min | 40% (SL), 30% (HL) |
| **Paper 3** | Mathematical questions | N/A | 1h | 20% (HL only) |
| **Internal Assessment** | Portfolio of 3 commentaries | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear and consistent communication of the topic and purpose throughout the introduction and body
Wellâstated hypothesis that aligns with the research question
Justification of the fiveâyear timeframe demonstrates IB rule compliance
weaknesses: Research question remains somewhat broad and occasionally drifts from industry focus
Methodology lacks detailed rationale for source selection and sampling
Introduction occasionally detaches from the stated research question
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows a solid grasp of focus and method: the topic and purpose are communicated effectively (band 5), but the questionâs scope and methodology detail fall short of the highest standard. Overall this performance merits a 3/6, reflecting clear strengths in framing and focus counterbalanced by gaps in question precision and methodological justification.

strengths: Relevant application of cultural, commercial and micro-economic source material in context
Appropriate use of supply and demand models to illustrate pricing contrasts
Integration of consumer-behavior concepts such as nostalgia and scarcity
weaknesses: Inconsistent definitions of key terms (elastic vs. inelastic demand) leading to conceptual confusion
Some statistical figures and diagrams lack clear provenance or productâspecific detail
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Knowledge and understanding are generally coherent and relevant (band 3â4), with good deployment of source material and concepts. However, recurring conceptual slips and insufficient precision in terminology and data attribution limit performance to the mid band, warranting 3/6.

strengths: Most research drawn is directly relevant to Starbucksâ pumpkin-spice case
Arguments are structured and acknowledge data limitations
Evaluation section recognizes competing explanations (nostalgia vs. scarcity)
weaknesses: Analysis remains largely qualitative with no explicit PED calculation or plotted data points
Critical evaluation is modest, with limited alternative interpretation
Conclusions are sometimes circular or unsupported by numerical evidence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Critical thinking exhibits clear relevance and a coherent argument structure (band 4â6) but falls short in analytical depth and critical evaluation. The lack of quantitative elasticity analysis and scant empirical plotting place this work in the lower half of the mid band, justifying a score of 2/12.

strengths: Report follows the expected Extended Essay structure with logical headings and contents page
In-text citations and visuals are used to support the narrative
weaknesses: Inconsistent or missing page numbering and irregular figure numbering
Several layout conventions (image resolution, referencing style) are unevenly applied
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation is logically organized and meets many formal requirements (band 3â4), but erratic pagination, low-resolution visuals, and inconsistent formatting conventions prevent flawless execution. This performance aligns with a mid-band score of 2/4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is clearly worded and appears at the start, but its scope is somewhat broad (linking Starbucksâ niche product to the whole coffee-industryâs strategies) and not fully narrowed to a testable economic variable set.  Connections to later discussion are present yet unevenâanalysis often reverts to Starbucks alone without industry comparisonâso the focus is partial rather than sustained.

The student states the topic and contextualises it effectively in the title, introduction, and subsequent sections, making clear that the essay will analyse seasonal demand for Starbucksâ pumpkin-spice products using micro-economic tools.  The purposeâgauging how seasonal demand and pricing strategies interactâis explicit and remains visible throughout, so focus and appropriateness are strong.  Minor breadth issues (e.g., occasional drift to general âcoffee-industryâ comments) prevent the very top of the band, hence 5 rather than 6.

Most quotations, statistics and graphs cited relate directly to price, sales, and elasticity, so relevance is generally sound. However, several figures lack clear provenance or calculation, and some stock diagrams are generic rather than product-specific, reducing the strength of application.  Hence âmostly relevantâ rather than âclearlyâ.

The report follows the expected EE structure, includes a contents page and logical headings, and flows sensibly. A few elements (figure numbering, some section pagination) are irregular, so it achieves clear appropriateness but not flawless executionâhence the lower half of the top band.

Basic formatting requirementsâtitle, word count, tables, in-text citationsâare met, and visuals are inserted. Yet page numbers are missing/erratic, some images are low-resolution, and referencing style is inconsistent, indicating that several layout conventions are applied incorrectly.

A structured evaluation section recognises data limitations and marketing biases, and the essay periodically weighs competing explanations (nostalgia vs scarcity). However, depth of critique is modest and alternative interpretations are not fully pursued. The argument is reasoned with partial critical evaluationâsquarely within the 4-6 band, at 4.

The essay analyses price trends, foot-traffic spikes and marketing tactics, linking them to theoretical models; arguments are coherent but largely qualitative and sometimes circular (using high sales to âproveâ inelasticity without numerical PED). Conclusions are present but only moderately supported, aligning with âclear analysis with mostly supported conclusionsâ.

The student demonstrates a reasonable grasp of micro-economic concepts (elasticity, shifts in demand, scarcity marketing) and links them to the case.  Yet there are conceptual slipsâconfusing inelastic with âelastic but feels inelasticâ, limited quantification of PEDâwhich signal only partial mastery.  Knowledge is coherent but not consistently accurate enough for the top band.

Research draws on numerous contemporary articles and data portals; most sources relate directly to Starbucksâ pumpkin-spice sales, so relevance is clear, though reliability varies and primary evidence is scarce. This matches the descriptor where a majority of research is appropriate and clearly relevant.

A wide list of secondary sources is shown and the student describes contacting Starbucks, but primary data are minimal and the selection rationale is only briefly mentioned. Methods (price tracking, secondary sales figures) are relevant yet lightly explained and limitations are acknowledged late.  Overall the range is adequate and partly informed, meriting the mid band.

Economic terms such as âequilibriumâ, âprice elasticityâ, âscarcityâ and âbandwagonâ are used frequently and generally correctly. Nevertheless, repeated contradictions about elasticity and occasional imprecise phrasing (ânear inelasticâ, âslightly elasticâ) impair consistency, placing performance in the middle band.

annotations.comment: The title effectively communicates the focus on seasonal demand and pricing strategies in a niche market, establishing clear purpose and focus.
The research question attempts to link Starbucksâ niche seasonal product to the broader coffee-industry strategies, making its scope too broad and diluting the focus.
The word count is clearly stated and falls within the IB limit, demonstrating compliance with formatting requirements.
The table of contents lacks consistent page numbering for each section, impairing navigability and structure clarity.
The footer area is blank and does not include page numbers, which is a formatting requirement for the extended essay.
The introduction provides a rich cultural background of the pumpkin spice phenomenon but doesnât explicitly link back to the research question.
The student demonstrates coherent knowledge of the cultural and commercial context, effectively applying relevant source material to the introduction.
Consider including precise page numbers alongside each entry in the contents table to improve professional presentation and user navigation.
Consider summarizing the methodology at the end of the introduction to clarify how data will be collected and analyzed.
The justification of the 2019â2024 timeframe clearly explains adherence to the IBâs five-year rule, strengthening the methodologyâs focus.
The methodology description mentions secondary sources and contacts at Starbucks but lacks detail on source selection criteria and sampling methods.
Elaborate on how primary contacts were selected and provide details of any questionnaires or archival data accessed to strengthen the methodology.
The hypothesis is clearly stated and aligns directly with the research question, setting a testable expectation for elasticity.
The hypothesis should specify the quantitative measure (e.g., PED formula) and data thresholds to enable rigorous testing.
The supply and demand model is applied appropriately to contrast latte pricing, demonstrating relevant microeconomic understanding.
The supply and demand curves are described generically; no actual priceâquantity data points are plotted, weakening the empirical analysis.
Incorporate actual price and quantity figures into the demandâsupply diagram to strengthen the linkage between theory and case data.
Figure 1 enhances the discussion of price inelasticity by providing a visual reference to the described trends.
The narrative discusses elasticity qualitatively but omits any explicit $$PED = \frac{\%\Delta Q}{\%\Delta P}$$ calculation, limiting analytical depth.
Provide numeric elasticity estimates based on your sales and price data to support the qualitative discussion.
The application of consumer behavior concepts such as nostalgia and scarcity is well integrated and relevant to the pumpkin spice phenomenon.
The text repeatedly refers to the product as both elastic and inelastic without precise definitions, causing conceptual confusion.
Clarify key economic terminology by defining elastic and inelastic demand explicitly to avoid contradictory statements.
The data analysis effectively highlights the distinct price differentials between pumpkin spice and regular lattes over a five-year period.
The claimed 79% price difference is presented without accompanying charts or step-by-step calculations, limiting transparency.
